subject: Human Resources
title: Offboarding and Exit
description: Learn Offboarding and Exit in one minute.

hook.text: An employee leaves on bad terms. They still have system access, company equipment, and client contacts.

buildup.text: Offboarding is the process of managing an employee's departure—voluntary or involuntary—professionally.

discovery.text: It includes revoking access, returning equipment, knowledge transfer, exit interviews, and final pay compliance.

twist.text: How you treat departing employees shapes your reputation. Alumni become future referrals—or vocal critics.

climax.text: Structured offboarding protects the company, preserves relationships, and captures institutional knowledge.

punchline.text: The last day matters as much as the first.

quiz.question: Why is structured offboarding important?
options: It protects the company and preserves relationships
It's only needed for fired employees
It has no impact on the company
